seed.name: Manager_Leave_Email_Responder_MultiProject_v0.1
seed.goal: Your agent acts as an intelligent email responder for your manager, who has stepped away unexpectedly due to a family emergency. Multiple important clients may email simultaneously about different ongoing projects. Each project has its own history, tone, and commitments. The agent must recognize which project is being referenced and reply with the correct context for that project.
seed.synthetic_domain:
Your agent acts as an intelligent email responder for your manager, who has stepped away unexpectedly due to a family emergency. Multiple important clients may email simultaneously about different ongoing projects. Each project has its own history, tone, and commitments. The agent must recognize which project is being referenced and reply with the correct context for that project.

Expanded scenario and context (for synthetic data generation and downstream evaluation):

A. Scenario framing

You are an AI-powered executive assistant embedded in the Business Development team at Trey Research (treyresearch.net), a mid-sized B2B consulting firm specializing in digital transformation projects for retailers and manufacturers. Your direct manager, Robin Doyle (Director of Business Development), is suddenly unavailable due to an urgent family emergency and is not expected to be reachable for the next 48 hours. During this window, several high-profile clients—including Northwind Traders (northwindtraders.com), Lakeshore Retail (lakeshore-retail.com), and Adatum Corporation (adatum.com)—may send urgent emails regarding active consulting projects. Each client project has unique deliverables, histories, communication styles, and outstanding commitments.

The primary objective is to recognize, from incoming emails, which ongoing project (and client) is being referenced, and respond promptly with accurate, context-aware replies that maintain appropriate tone and clarity. The business risk is high: these relationships are in critical phases (e.g., pilot launches, pre-renewal negotiations, troubleshooting of major incidents), and failure to respond correctly may jeopardize client trust or contractual milestones. The agent’s core responsibility is to serve as a responsive and reliable proxy, replying to client emails with the right context, status, and next steps for the referenced project, until your manager returns.

B. High-level task:
- Monitor the executive’s inbox for incoming client emails, prioritizing those from key accounts.
- Analyze message content to accurately identify which project and client the email pertains to, even when project names or details are subtle or omitted.
- Retrieve relevant project context (timeline, commitments, deliverables, recent conversations) from email history and the internal project tracker.
- Draft and send reply emails tailored to each project/client’s style and history, referencing appropriate context and current status.
- Clearly communicate any dependencies, next steps, or interim actions, and relay that the executive is temporarily unavailable.
- Flag and escalate truly urgent or ambiguous messages per company policy.
- Success is judged by accuracy of project referencing, clarity and appropriateness of responses, and the minimization of confusion or errors in client communication.

C. Organizations and environment:
- Primary organization: Trey Research (treyresearch.net)
  - B2B digital consulting firm, 200 employees (mid-stage growth).
  - Provides digital transformation, business process optimization, and technology implementation projects for retail and manufacturing clients.
  - Serves North America and Europe; HQ in America/Chicago (CST), with project teams in multiple time zones.
- Counterpart organizations:
  - Northwind Traders (northwindtraders.com): major wholesale distributor in consumer goods; current project in logistics optimization.
  - Lakeshore Retail (lakeshore-retail.com): regional brick-and-mortar and e-commerce retailer; current client in customer engagement analytics pilot.
  - Adatum Corporation (adatum.com): global manufacturer of smart devices; current client in post-implementation support escalation.
- Business context:
  - Several projects are approaching critical review points (phase go-lives, issue resolution, pre-renewal periods).
  - High stakes: miscommunication could cause delays, revenue impact, or relationship damage.
  - Executive’s unavailability is not yet publicly known; maintaining seamless cover is essential.
- Core systems involved:
  - Corporate email (main channel).
  - Internal project tracker with status, deadlines, commitments (accessible to AI assistant).
  - CRM platform for relationship context and contact history.

D. Personas and stakeholders:
- Robin Doyle, Director of Business Development (Trey Research) – Primary owner of executive inbox [primary, America/Chicago], prefers concise status updates, avoids jargon, email: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net.
- Peyton Davis, Senior Project Manager (Trey Research) – Secondary, supports on logistics, prefers full details only when asked, email: peyton.davis@treyresearch.net.
- Sawyer Miller, VP Operations (Northwind Traders) – Client stakeholder [primary], expects direct actionable updates, America/New_York, email: sawyer.miller@northwindtraders.com.
- Taylor Winslow, Customer Insights Lead (Lakeshore Retail) – Client, semi-formal tone, prefers responses within 2 business hours, America/Denver, email: taylor.winslow@lakeshore-retail.com.
- Amelie Garner, Director of IT (Adatum Corporation) – Client, expects highly technical clarity, prefers follow-ups grouped by thread, Europe/Berlin, email: amelie.garner@adatum.com.
- Mia Steele, Account Executive (Trey Research) – Optional, supports on client renewals, prefers being CC’d rather than direct replies, email: mia.steele@treyresearch.net.

E. Data sources and artifacts for synthetic data:
- Incoming emails: varied in style, may refer obliquely to project specifics or people (“as discussed on the analytics pilot,” “per yesterday’s call...”).
- Email threads: typically 2–8 messages with quoted prior context, mix of formal/informal, some with forwarded chain noise.
- Project tracker summaries: structured sections summarizing key milestones, next actions, deadlines, internal owners.
- CRM contact notes: free-text logs of previous commitments, personality notes (e.g., preferred tone, escalation rules).
- Occasionally: attachments (spreadsheets, status reports, timelines), sometimes referenced but missing.
- Expect diversity in reference styles (formal project code vs nickname; named vs unnamed deliverables).

F. Operational constraints and requirements:
- Business rules:
  - Only reply as proxy for Robin Doyle; do not create new commitments or approve out-of-scope requests.
  - Mention Robin’s unavailability only if strictly necessary, using discreet wording.
  - Escalate urgent issues marked “critical” or with time-sensitive language to Peyton Davis.
- Time constraints:
  - Respond to VIP clients (Northwind Traders, Lakeshore Retail, Adatum) within 1 hour during working hours (08:30–18:00 local time).
- Quality constraints:
  - Use client-appropriate tone (semi-formal for Lakeshore, direct for Northwind, technical for Adatum).
  - Replies must reference accurate, up-to-date project details; do not bluff or guess facts.
  - Do not reference sensitive internal-only comments.
- Compliance/privacy:
  - No PII leakage; keep responses corporate appropriate.

G. Edge and negative cases to include in synthetic data:
- Emails that vaguely allude to “the deliverable” without specifying which project or milestone.
- Multiple clients emailing about different projects within overlapping timeframes and using similar terminology.
- Client refers to a conversation or document that is not present in the email thread or attachments.
- Incoming email with contradictory instructions vs. what’s logged in the project tracker.
- Misdirected emails (addressed to the manager but concerning a project not owned by Trey Research).
- Client asks for an immediate new commitment or offer: agent must politely defer, citing unavailability of decision-maker.
- Sudden escalation: “If we don’t receive X by end of today, we may need to reconsider our renewal.”
- Informal emails using nicknames/slang for projects (“the Lightning Run thing”) requiring disambiguation.
- Email threads that include internal side threads not relevant to the client reply.
- Requests in languages other than English (e.g., a greeting or sign-off in German for Amelie Garner).
- Client forwards conversation with a different Trey Research team; agent must clarify scope before responding.
- Stakeholder disagreement or conflicting asks (“Sawyer said to delay, but Peyton confirmed go-live”).
- Attachments referenced in the email are missing from the received message.

H. Input characteristics for synthetic data:
- Mix of direct asks (“Status update on logistics tracker?”) and verbose, context-heavy emails.
- Variability in tone: formal (Adatum), direct (Northwind), semi-casual (Lakeshore), with occasional typos or abbreviations (“pls adv ASAP,” “LMK re: pilot”).
- Incomplete or ambiguous project references, e.g., “the Q3 deliverable,” “that issue from last week.”
- Occasional short greetings or phrases in other languages (e.g., “Guten Morgen,” “Ciao for now”).
- Quoted threads may contain unrelated prior emails or internal banter between Trey Research colleagues.
- Example subject lines/openers:
  - “Quick check: Analytics pilot status?”
  - “Follow-up on our last call (project Lightning Run)”
  - “URGENT: logistics escalation – need update”
  - “Missing attachment for phase 2 review”
  - “FYI: rescheduling?”
- Some emails may violate common templates, lack salutations, or use inconsistent sign-off format.

I. Expected outputs (conceptual, for evaluation and synthetic data alignment):
- Structured object per reply:
  - Recipient and project identified (client, project/milestone reference).
  - Referenced commitments and status (pulled from tracker and CRM notes).
  - Reply draft: appropriate tone, accurate context, close any gaps (e.g., request clarification if not enough info).
- Key fields for evaluators:
  - Correct mapping of email to project/client.
  - Response completeness and appropriateness (right details, tone, and coverage of request).
  - Escalation/deferral as required by content.
- Human-readable email outputs:
  - Subject line (mirroring/replying to incoming email).
  - Polite, concise reply body, structured in short paragraphs or bullet points.
  - Clear communication re: executive’s temporary unavailability and next steps; requests for clarifications when unsure.
  - Alignment between structured object fields and text content.

J. Behavioral expectations (for downstream evaluation, not tool prescription):
- Correctly identify referenced project/client from subtle or oblique email clues.
- Use and reference latest, accurate data from project tracker and CRM.
- Apply appropriate tone and level of detail to each client/context.
- Maintain business rules: do not overcommit or reveal internal-only info.
- Escalate ambiguous, truly urgent, or conflicting requests.
- Politely clarify when insufficient information is given; never assume or bluff critical details.
- Handles noisy, multi-threaded email conversations; avoids responding to irrelevant internal banter.
- Avoids double replying or missing genuinely urgent requests.
- Declines or defers out-of-scope asks without alienating client.
- Communicates status of executive’s unavailability only as required, in sensitive language.
- Does not hallucinate commitments; maintains factual accuracy and referential alignment.

K. Content guidelines for synthetic data:
- CRITICAL: Use ONLY company names and domains from the provided fictional companies list:
  - All company and domain references above are from the supplied list (e.g., treyresearch.net, northwindtraders.com, lakeshore-retail.com, adatum.com).
  - All corporate emails use the format: firstname.lastname@companydomain.
- Do NOT invent new company names or domains.
- Personas, organizations, emails, time zones, data sources, and business context are aligned and internally consistent.
- Numbers, dates, deadlines, and project phases must be plausible and realistic.
- Input samples (emails) mix formal and semi-casual business English, with sporadic informal wordings, typos, or brief multilingual sign-offs in accordance with persona or company context.
- Never mention real product/tool names in scenario or sample data—refer to internal or generic systems only.
- Provide enough detail in all scenario elements to enable generation of richly varied synthetic emails, project tracker summaries, and CRM context for robust downstream evaluation.

test_cases.name: Reply to Sawyer Miller with Project Status Email
test_cases.description: Respond to a clearly-worded urgent email from Sawyer Miller (Northwind Traders) regarding the logistics optimization project, with all needed project details accessible from the latest email thread and project tracker. Focus is on recognizing the project from explicit cues and delivering a concise, context-appropriate status update with no missing information.
test_cases.input: Sawyer Miller from Northwind Traders has just sent you a clearly worded urgent email about the logistics optimization project. Read his message, recognize which project he’s referring to from the cues he gives, and pull any missing details from the latest email thread and the project tracker. Then reply to him with a concise, professional status update that fits the situation and covers all of the information he needs, without leaving anything important out.

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: URGENT: Logistics Optimization Project - Status and Missing Phase 2 Review Attachment

Hi Robin,

I need your immediate attention on the logistics optimization project. We still haven't received the final Phase 2 review doc that was supposed to be shared last Friday.

> On Mon, Jun 10, 2024 at 3:18 PM, Robin Doyle <robin.doyle@treyresearch.net> wrote:
> Hi Sawyer, confirming we’re running validation on the last-mile route scenarios and final summary should be sent by end of week. CC’ing Mia for visibility.

We’re facing a blocker on our side until that report is delivered, as our operations and procurement teams require the updated routing recommendations before signing off. Is there an updated ETA for the document?  We’re under strict internal deadlines, and this has become a critical issue.

Please confirm whether the document is available. If it was sent already, I did not see any attachment in your previous email—can you resend the file?

CC’ing Peyton, as this escalates our internal delivery timeline risk. Any delay will directly affect our Q3 rollout schedule with key retail partners.

Let me know asap if you're able to share the review today, or if there’s another contact who can provide it in Robin’s absence.

Best,
Sawyer

---
> On Fri, Jun 7, 2024 at 1:05 PM, Mia Steele <mia.steele@treyresearch.net> wrote:
> Sawyer, confirming receipt of your feedback on milestone completion. We'll incorporate the updates and revert with the consolidated review doc.

---
> On Thu, Jun 6, 2024 at 11:30 AM, Sawyer Miller <sawyer.miller@northwindtraders.com> wrote:
> Thanks, Mia. Our steering group is requesting the compiled Phase 2 logistics optimization deliverables before COB Friday, as previously agreed. Please advise on progress and forward the full report when ready.

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (9 checks); Content requirements met (7 checks); Execution flow verified (3 checks). Total: 19 quality assertions.

test_cases.name: Reply to Taylor Winslow with Project Status and Clarification Request via Email
test_cases.description: Reply to a verbose, semi-casual message from Taylor Winslow (Lakeshore Retail) about the analytics pilot that only refers obliquely to project details (e.g., 'our dashboard thing from last week'). Requires disambiguating the project using recent internal notes, and politely requesting any needed clarification if information is insufficient, while delivering as much accurate status as possible.
test_cases.input: Taylor Winslow at Lakeshore Retail has sent you a long, semi-casual email about the analytics pilot, but she only refers to it indirectly (for example, as ‘our dashboard thing from last week’). Read her message, use recent internal notes and context to pin down exactly which project and workstream she means, and then draft a reply. In your reply, share as much accurate status as you can, and if anything is still ambiguous or missing, politely ask Taylor for clarification while keeping the tone friendly and professional.

assertion: SearchMessages 'queryString' argument contains 'from:taylor.winslow@lakeshoreretail.com' and analytics/dashboard keywords

assertion: Argument 'sender' is provided and valid

sendMail 'body' argument escalates any urgent or ambiguous issues to Peyton Davis per business rules

arguments.name: subject
assertion: sendMail 'subject' argument is provided, non-empty, and obliquely references analytics pilot or dashboard (e.g., 'Analytics Dashboard Pilot – Project Status and Clarification')
sendMail 'subject' argument may contain informal phrasing or typos consistent with Taylor Winslow's style

arguments.name: body
assertion: sendMail 'body' argument is provided, non-empty, and contains current analytics pilot project status
sendMail 'body' argument length is at least 300 characters and includes quoted prior thread context
sendMail 'body' argument includes requests for clarification if project context is ambiguous or missing critical details

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: quick check on the dashboard thing from last week (pilot, etc?)

Hi Robin,

Hope you’re holding up! Just wanted to do a quick check-in about our dashboard (the *pilot thing* from last week — still calling it that, ha). Did you see Mia’s note about the metrics glitch? Not sure if it’s something on our side, but I can ask the team if needed. Let me know if you need screenshots, etc!

Also, was Peyton able to review the engagement numbers that got reshuffled Friday? I thought those would be in the new view on the dashboard, but I'm still not seeing the breakdown by region, just by store. (Apologies if I'm looking in the wrong place — Mondays!

Here's a snippet from Mia’s email in the thread, just FYI:

---
> *From: Mia B, lakeshore-retail.com*
> *Sent: Friday, November 8, 2025 2:39 PM*
> *To: Taylor Winslow*
> *Subject: Re: Dashboard pilot updates (FW: Metrics)*
>
> “Hey, I made a few notes on the dashboard re: region grouping — looks like the pilot view reset some of our filters. Let Taylor know if there’s an updated doc or if I need to flag anything in Teams. Didn’t have time to check attachments yet, sry!”

---

Not urgent (unless the metrics glitch blows up — in which case I’ll escalate to Peyton), but would be great to get eyes on it before Thursday’s sync. If you need anything from Lakeshore side, ping me (WhatsApp or email, doesn’t matter).

BTW, any word on the attachments you mentioned in your last reply? Haven't seen those come through yet, just in case it’s a server thing.

Thanks as always — holler if you need anything else or if I missed a memo.

À bientôt,
Taylor

---
> *On Thu, Nov 7, 2025 at 12:05 PM, Robin Doyle <robin.doyle@treyresearch.net> wrote:*
>
> "Hi Taylor & Mia,
>
> We pushed some updates to the dashboard pilot yesterday — should make the engagement numbers easier to slice per region, as discussed with Peyton. If you run into any glitches, let us know before Thursday and we’ll loop Peyton in.
>
> I’ll share updated doc as soon as it’s finalized (still pending — waiting on our internal review to wrap up). Hang tight!
>
> Cheers, Robin"
---

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (3 checks); Content requirements met (6 checks); Execution flow verified (6 checks). Total: 15 quality assertions.

tc_tc-drafting-email-2_mock_1.title: Project Tracker Record: Customer Engagement Analytics Pilot - Lakeshore Retail

tc_tc-drafting-email-2_mock_2.title: Lakeshore Retail Analytics Pilot – Recent Contact History, Commitments, and Persona Preferences (Internal Reference)
tc_tc-drafting-email-2_mock_2.content_md:
# Lakeshore Retail Analytics Pilot – Internal Reference Document

## Contact History with Taylor Winslow and Lakeshore Stakeholders

- **2024-06-05**  
  • Kick-off call for engagement analytics pilot; established project scope, deliverables, and initial dashboard approach.  
  • **Participants:** taylor.winslow@lakeshore-retail.com, robin.doyle@treyresearch.net, peyton.davis@treyresearch.net

- **2024-06-10**  
  • Weekly check-in (virtual meeting); Taylor provided feedback on customer segmentation logic and requested more granular filter options.  
  • **Participants:** taylor.winslow@lakeshore-retail.com, robin.doyle@treyresearch.net

- **2024-06-14**  
  • Urgent follow-up initiated by Taylor querying status of dashboard refresh timing after noted data lag.  
  • **Participants:** taylor.winslow@lakeshore-retail.com, robin.doyle@treyresearch.net

- **2024-06-18**  
  • Milestone hand-off: shared initial dashboard iteration covering customer engagement metrics; invited Lakeshore team for review and feedback.  
  • **Participants:** taylor.winslow@lakeshore-retail.com, robin.doyle@treyresearch.net, blake.martin@lakeshore-retail.com

## Commitments and Open Items

- **Description:** Adjust dashboard filters for customer segmentation per Taylor’s 6/10 feedback.  
  **Status:** pending  
  **Origin Date:** 2024-06-10  
  **Expected Resolution Date:** 2024-06-19  

- **Description:** Address urgent concern about data lag in dashboard refresh (see 6/14 follow-up); review update schedule and advise.  
  **Status:** escalated  
  **Origin Date:** 2024-06-14  
  **Expected Resolution Date:** 2024-06-16  
  **Escalation Notes:** Escalated to Peyton Davis for urgent business impact assessment and resolution coordination per Lakeshore escalation protocol.

- **Description:** Deliver initial dashboard iteration for pilot review.  
  **Status:** resolved  
  **Origin Date:** 2024-06-05  
  **Expected Resolution Date:** 2024-06-18  

## Taylor Winslow Persona Preferences (from CRM Log)

- **Tone/Style:** semi-formal (occasionally semi-casual for status; prefers clear, professional communication)
- **Response Time Expectation:** within 2 business hours
- **Special Notes:**  
  • Appreciates concise status updates; prefers bullet points for clarity  
  • Dislikes excessive jargon and technical detail unless specifically requested  
  • Favors direct call-outs of open action items and resolved deliverables

---
*Internal Reference Document – Generated for agent review*
sections: Contact History with Taylor Winslow and Lakeshore Stakeholders
Taylor Winslow Persona Preferences (from CRM Log)

test_cases.name: Draft Context-Aware Email Reply Reconciling Issue Ownership
test_cases.description: Craft a context-aware response to Amelie Garner (Adatum Corporation), who references technical troubleshooting but uses a mix of English and a German greeting in her urgent thread. The project tracker contains conflicting information about issue ownership, requiring the agent to reconcile details, reference the latest status, and escalate to Peyton Davis if urgency or ambiguity is detected.
test_cases.input: Amelie Garner from Adatum Corporation has started an urgent email thread where she’s describing a technical troubleshooting issue, opening with a German greeting but otherwise writing in English. Read through her thread and the related project tracker entries, notice that the tracker has conflicting information about who owns the issue, and reconcile that information so you can figure out the correct owner. Then write a context-aware reply to Amelie that reflects the latest status, clarifies ownership, and, if the situation is urgent or ambiguous, escalates appropriately to Peyton Davis while still sounding clear and professional to Amelie.

arguments.name: queryString
assertion: SearchMessages 'queryString' argument contains 'from:amelie.garner@adatum.com' and urgency-related keywords ('support', 'troubleshooting', 'escalation')
SearchMessages 'queryString' argument for second call contains 'project tracker', 'ownership', or 'escalation' and sender context

arguments.name: sender
assertion: SearchMessages 'queryString' argument for second call contains 'project tracker', 'ownership', or 'escalation' and sender context

assertion: SearchMessages 'size' argument is set and limits results to 20 or fewer

arguments.name: enableTopResults
assertion: SearchMessages 'enableTopResults' argument is true to prioritize relevant urgent messages

sendMail 'body' argument acknowledges Robin Doyle’ unavailability per policy and escalates issue to Peyton Davis
sendMail 'body' argument length is at least 200 characters to capture quoted thread and technical content

assertion: Argument 'bcc' is provided and valid

arguments.name: subject
assertion: sendMail 'subject' argument is provided, non-empty, and contains escalation/support/ownership keywords along with German greeting ('Sehr geehrte Frau Garner', 'Guten Morgen')

arguments.name: body
assertion: sendMail 'body' argument is provided and matches thread context: references urgent issue, technical troubleshooting, post-implementation support, ambiguity/conflicting ownership, and multilingual elements (English/German)
sendMail 'body' argument acknowledges Robin Doyle’ unavailability per policy and escalates issue to Peyton Davis
sendMail 'body' argument length is at least 200 characters to capture quoted thread and technical content

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: URGENT: Critical Post-Go-Live Issue Escalation – Guten Morgen

Guten Morgen Robin,

I'm escalating what appears to be a **critical production outage** impacting several Adatum internal users following our go-live on the Supply Chain Integration project.

---
**Issue Details**
- **Symptom:** User authentication failures across multiple modules (Finance, Operations).
- **Observed Since:** 12:46 AM UTC, today.
- **Temporary Workaround:** Clearing cached credentials (per support email from lakeshore-retail.com contact) is NOT effective.
- **Impact:** Affects all post-migration reporting; executive dashboard unavailable.

—
**Troubleshooting Summary:**
- Our internal team traced logs suggesting misrouting of SSO tokens.
- The issue appears in the `status_report_Q3.pdf` deliverable (attached in your update, now missing in the last email). 
- Ownership in the project tracker lists both "Robin Doyle (Trey Research)" and "Peyton Davis (Trey Research)" as escalation contacts, but Peyton's last reply (see below) says you are primary POC. 

**We require immediate clarification and action.**

—
Reference thread for context (quoted inline):

---
*On 2025-11-13T04:07Z, Peyton Davis (peyton.davis@treyresearch.net) wrote:*

> Hi Amelie and team,
> 
> Thanks for flagging this. Per the updated tracker, Robin is listed as lead for all post-go-live support escalations (see attached `issue_log.xlsx`)—though I will monitor for urgent blockers.
> 
> Amelie, can you confirm when you first saw user failures?

---
*On 2025-11-12T23:51Z, Robin Doyle (robin.doyle@treyresearch.net) wrote:*

> Amelie,
>
> Uploading `status_report_Q3.pdf` with detailed timeline. If downstream systems are failing repeat authentication, we may need Adatum to reset trust anchors.
>
> Confirm receipt of the attachment—let me know if there are missing files (Teams upload had sync issues earlier).

---
*On 2025-11-12T22:37Z, Peyton Davis (peyton.davis@treyresearch.net) replied:*

> Ownership assignment is unclear in the tracker post-handover. Robin, can you update the checkpoint table in the next sync?

---

Sorry for multiple threads—can you confirm current **issue owner** for this escalation? Please prioritize this and respond as soon as possible; our SLAs require critical incidents like this to be acknowledged within **1 hour**.

Let me know if Peyton or someone else should be included in further updates; I've copied her above for visibility.

Danke für Ihre schnelle Rückmeldung.

Kind regards,

Amelie Garner
Director of IT, Adatum Corporation

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (3 checks); Content requirements met (5 checks); Format constraints satisfied (1 checks); Execution flow verified (4 checks). Total: 13 quality assertions.

test_cases.name: Reply Separately to Simultaneous Client Escalation Emails
test_cases.description: Handle a time-sensitive escalation where two different clients (Northwind Traders and Lakeshore Retail) email near-simultaneously using similar terminology ('phase 2 deliverable'). The agent must correctly identify and map each email to its respective project, avoid cross-contamination of context, and deliver personalized, accurate replies meeting each client's expected tone and urgency.
test_cases.input: Two different clients—Northwind Traders and Lakeshore Retail—email you almost at the same time, both talking about a ‘phase 2 deliverable’ in language that sounds similar. Read each thread carefully, figure out which project each email belongs to, and make sure you don’t mix up or cross-contaminate the context between them. Then send back personalized, accurate replies to each client that match their expected tone and sense of urgency, while keeping the projects and deliverables clearly separated in your responses.

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: Requested Update: Phase 2 Deliverable Status & Potential Risks

Hi Robin,

Following up on our last call, I'm reaching out regarding the current status of the **Phase 2 deliverable** for the logistics workflow optimization project. As we approach our internal review milestone, I need clarity on whether we're on track for the originally agreed handover date this month.

There are a few open items our executive team flagged yesterday:
- The revised implementation report was referenced in the prior thread, but I haven't received the final document yet (was it sent separately?)
- We still require confirmation on system integration test completion, as delays here directly impact our Q4 renewal timeline
- If any additional blockers have come up since the last update, please flag them asap—the board is pressing for a contingency plan if slippage occurs

To avoid unnecessary escalation, could you confirm today if all critical dependencies are on schedule? If any major risks remain unresolved, let us know immediately—otherwise, our CFO has directed me to initiate a review with your senior program lead (cc: Peyton Davis) by end of week.

Let me know if you received the attached phase 2 report (or if it's missing on your end, please advise).

Thanks,

Sawyer Miller  
Director, Logistics Transformation  
Northwind Traders

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (7 checks); Content requirements met (6 checks); Execution flow verified (5 checks). Total: 18 quality assertions.

Sawyer Miller

tc_tc-drafting-email-4_mock_1.subject: Quick check-in: phase 2 deliverable progress?
tc_tc-drafting-email-4_mock_1.body_md:
Hi Robin,

Hope you're doing well. I'm checking in about the status of the phase 2 deliverable (a.k.a. the "Lightning Run" piece from our last sync). We're getting some internal pressure to confirm the handoff timeline—could you let me know if we're still on track for the revised ETA, or if there are any blockers?

If you have a draft or update, would you mind sending it my way? I noticed there was supposed to be a timeline attached to your last note, but I didn't see anything come through (might be on my end!).

> "Shipping out the next version by the end of week, details in attached timeline"

Let me know what the latest is, or if you're waiting on anything from our side. If it's easier, happy to jump on a call this afternoon—just let me know what works.

Would appreciate a reply by EOD if possible—thanks so much!

Best,
Taylor
Lakeshore Retail

test_cases.name: Defer Client Deliverable Approval via Email
test_cases.description: Receive an email from a client (Northwind Traders) requesting approval for a new out-of-scope deliverable. The agent must recognize the boundary of its proxy authority, gently defer the commitment citing Robin's brief unavailability, and refer the request for review, all without alienating the client or overstepping role constraints.
test_cases.input: You receive an email from a Northwind Traders contact asking for approval on a new deliverable that’s clearly outside the current project scope. Read their request, recognize that this goes beyond what you’re allowed to approve as a proxy, and draft a reply that gently defers making any commitment. In your response, mention that Robin is briefly unavailable, explain that the request will be reviewed, and make sure you stay within your role boundaries while still sounding helpful and keeping the client relationship positive.

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: Follow-up: Approval Needed for Q3 Logistics Optimization Add-On (Lightning Run)

Guten Morgen Robin,

Quick note to follow up on the conversation from earlier this week. Our team has started mapping out the additional dashboard module for the Lightning Run initiative – specifically, the shipment reroute optimization that Peyton mentioned would be useful for Q3.

To move forward on this, we *really* need green light from you (or at least your go-ahead for this month’s sprint). I know this wasn’t part of the initial project scope, but considering what’s at stake for pilot success, it feels critical – and the ops engineers are asking for a status so they’re not in limbo. If there’s anything I can clarify (or if you need extra docs), happy to hop on a call today or tomorrow. We’re hoping to wrap up that side by Friday EOB for internal review.

> Quoted message from Peyton Davis:
> “Just flagging for both of you – the additional dashboard is out-of-scope, so let’s confirm with Robin before any resources are assigned.”

Also – let me know if you had a chance to look over the requirements spreadsheet I sent Monday (should be in your inbox, but I can re-send if missing). No attachment this time, just circling back.

Sorry for the ping but our side considers this pretty urgent. Appreciate your review, Robin.

Thanks,
Sawyer

---

*Sent from my phone – apologies for brevity/any typos*

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (3 checks); Content requirements met (4 checks); Format constraints satisfied (1 checks); Execution flow verified (7 checks). Total: 15 quality assertions.

tc_tc-drafting-email-5_mock_2.title: CRM Contact Notes – Sawyer Miller / Northwind Traders – Logistics Optimization Project
tc_tc-drafting-email-5_mock_2.content_md:
# CRM Contact Notes – Sawyer Miller / Northwind Traders – Logistics Optimization Project

## Relationship Background

The collaboration between Trey Research (treyresearch.net) and Northwind Traders (northwindtraders.com) began in Q3 2023 with an initial discovery call on **September 14, 2023**, where Sawyer Miller (VP Operations, Northwind Traders) outlined their goals for logistics improvement. Major project milestones include:

1. **Phase 1: Needs Assessment** – Completed in October 2023 following joint workshops and process mapping sessions (reference: workshop notes dated October 23, 2023). 
2. **Phase 2: Pilot Implementation** – Launched January 18, 2024, culminating in a successful pilot milestone delivery on **February 28 – March 15, 2024**. Results presented at the March 19 project review call.
3. **Ongoing: Optimization & Issue Escalation** – Current stage is active implementation and tuning, transitioning toward Phase 3 post-pilot rollout. 

Recent Interactions:
- **June 12, 2024**: Strategy call with Sawyer and Trey Research team to address pilot feedback and set targets for full rollout.
- **June 24, 2024**: Email exchange regarding expedited reporting requests and escalation procedures.
- **June 28, 2024**: Sawyer raised questions about out-of-scope feature requests; deferred as per proxy policy (see Prior Commitments section).

The relationship is currently at the **pre-renewal and ongoing pilot optimization stage**, with emphasis on maintaining transparent communication and prompt issue resolution. Escalation protocols have been reinforced due to recent proxy coverage for Robin Doyle (Director of Business Development), currently unavailable.

## Contact Preferences – Sawyer Miller

- **Communication Style:** Prefers direct, actionable updates rather than lengthy, detailed explanations. Feedback from CRM notes (June 12 call): "Appreciates bullet points and summaries over full reports."
- **Urgency Thresholds:** Expects replies **within 1 hour** during Eastern Time workdays (reference: June 24 email thread – "Please LMK ASAP if delays."
- **Escalation Rules:** Only mark requests as urgent if they impact critical business outcomes. Past note: "Urgent requests should be flagged only if delivery will affect distribution schedules."
- **Tone:** Appreciates a professional but **not overly formal** approach. Respond positively to short, actionable subject lines (e.g., "Delivery ETA Update"), avoids passive wording.
- **Language Cues:** Frequently uses abbreviations (e.g., ASAP, LMK) in emails. Reacts best to bullet points and numbered lists in responses. CRM entry from Mar 19, 2024: "Sawyer welcomed use of clear bullet points for next steps."
- **Multilingual Communication:** Accepts greetings and closings in **English or Spanish only**; prefers English for business content. Example: Opening with "Buenos días" or "Good morning" is welcomed.

## Prior Commitments and Special Instructions

- **Outstanding Deliverables:**
  - Provide Phase 2 logistics optimization report to Sawyer Miller by **July 18, 2024** (committed at June 12 strategy call).
  - Share updated rollout timeline for Phase 3 by **July 22, 2024** (per June 24 email).
- **Deferral/Escalation Procedures:**
  - All out-of-scope requests (e.g., feature additions outside current SOW) must be deferred to **Peyton Davis** (Project Manager) when **Robin Doyle** is unavailable (noted at June 28 CRM entry).
- **Proxy Authority Limitations:**
  - Do **not** approve new deliverables unless confirmed by Robin Doyle; refer to Peyton Davis in interim per proxy instruction (June 24 escalation thread).
- **Sensitivity Markers:**
  - Avoid discussing internal staffing or resource issues with Sawyer; flagged as sensitive in May 2024 CRM update.
- **Examples of Prior Situations:**
  - **June 2024:** Request for expedited feature change (June 28) was deferred, redirected to Peyton Davis; client accepted handling as per established protocol.
  - **March 2024:** Timeline adjustment request routed through official channel, resolved without overcommitting by proxy.

**Instruction for Handling New Requests:**
- Acknowledge all requests promptly.
- Defer or escalate any out-of-scope asks per above.
- Do not commit to changes or new deliverables without primary stakeholder approval.
- Maintain professional tone, cite proxy coverage only when necessary, and reassure continuity.

---

*Document Author: Mia Steele, CRM Analyst (Trey Research)*
*Created: 2024-06-29*
*Last Updated: 2024-06-29*

## Metadata
- **Client Email:** sawyer.miller@northwindtraders.com
- **Project Code:** NWT-LOGISTICS-OPTIMIZATION

test_cases.name: Draft Email Reply Acknowledging Missing Timeline Attachment
test_cases.description: Respond to a forwarded, cluttered thread where the client references a missing attachment ('timeline.xlsx') and includes side conversations from Trey Research staff. The AI must filter out unrelated internal banter, recognize the referenced project despite incomplete cues, and draft a polite reply that acknowledges the missing document, assures follow-up, and requests any needed clarification.
test_cases.input: You’ve been forwarded a cluttered email thread where the client mentions a missing attachment called ‘timeline.xlsx,’ but the thread is full of side conversations and internal chatter from Trey Research staff. Read through the thread, ignore the unrelated banter, and use the available cues to figure out which project the client is actually talking about. Then write a polite reply that acknowledges the missing timeline.xlsx file, reassures the client that you’ll follow up to get it to them, and asks for any clarifications you still need—while keeping the message clear, professional, and easy for the client to act on.

arguments.name: queryString
assertion: SearchMessages 'queryString' argument is provided and non-empty
SearchMessages 'queryString' contains 'subject:timeline.xlsx' and client domains (northwindtraders.com, adatum.com, lakeshoreretail.com)

arguments.name: to
assertion: sendMail 'to' argument is provided and contains 'contact@northwindtraders.com'
sendMail 'subject' contains reference to 'timeline.xlsx' and project context (e.g., 'Re: Project Timeline – Missing Attachment')
sendMail 'body' uses polite and professional tone suitable for client communication from Trey Research executive

arguments.name: subject
assertion: sendMail 'subject' argument is provided and non-empty
sendMail 'subject' contains reference to 'timeline.xlsx' and project context (e.g., 'Re: Project Timeline – Missing Attachment')

arguments.name: body
assertion: sendMail 'body' argument is provided and non-empty
sendMail 'body' content length is at least 100 characters
sendMail 'body' includes acknowledgement of missing 'timeline.xlsx' attachment

test_cases.expected_response:
To: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net
Subject: Fwd: Analytics Pilot (Run) - Timeline.xlsx Missing from Thread

Hallo Robin,

Ich hoffe, Sie hatten einen guten Wochenstart. Ich schreibe bezüglich unseres letzten Austauschs zur Analytics-Pilotphase für Adatum. In der vorigen Nachricht hatten Sie erwähnt, dass Sie eine aktualisierte Version der Projekt-Timeline bereitstellen würden („timeline.xlsx“), aber ich konnte die Datei im Thread nicht finden.

Könnten Sie bitte kurz bestätigen, ob die Datei vielleicht separat verschickt wurde oder noch aussteht? Wir wollen mit dem internen Validierungsteam bis Mittwoch weiterarbeiten und benötigen einen möglichst aktuellen Zeitplan. 

Falls es Änderungen am Rollout-Plan gibt oder wenn Sie ein anderes Dokument als Referenz meinten, geben Sie bitte Bescheid.

Danke und viele Grüße,
Amelie Garner

---

> Von: peyton.davis@treyresearch.net   
> An: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net   
> Betreff: Re: Analytics Pilot (Run) - Timeline / Internal logistics & jokes :)
>
> FYI: Sawyer still owes us coffee from the last meeting. Also, does anyone know if the catering invoice for next week was processed? (Not urgent)
>
> [Unrelated banter about hoopla at lunch -- omitted for customer-facing reply]

---

> Von: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net   
> An: amelie.garner@adatum.com   
> Betreff: Analytics Pilot (Run) - Timeline.xlsx coming soon!
>
> Hi Amelie,
>
> We will send you the most recent version of timeline.xlsx by EOD Tuesday. There are a couple of minor adjustments proposed for the second sprint, but nothing major expected.
>
> Should you need anything else, let me know. (Attachment referenced: timeline.xlsx)

---

> Von: amelie.garner@adatum.com   
> An: robin.doyle@treyresearch.net   
> Betreff: Quick check: Analytics pilot status.
>
> Hello Robin,
>
> Just a quick check-in: wanted to confirm if there's any go/no-go update on the analytics pilot, or if we're still green for Thursday's checkpoint. (No urgency, but if docs changed let us know.)

response_quality_expectation.assertion: Response structure validated (2 checks); Content requirements met (4 checks); Format constraints satisfied (1 checks); Execution flow verified (6 checks). Total: 13 quality assertions.
